task: Write a Python function longest_word(s) returning the longest whitespace-separated word in the string. On ties return the last such word, not the first. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the criteria.
trace: I chose to split the input string into words and then iterate over them in reverse order. This allows me to easily handle ties by returning the last word with the maximum length, as required. The function first checks if the list of words is empty and returns an empty string if so, handling the edge case where the input is an empty string.
